zh.lifeSummary: 當William FitzNigel de Stafford de Gresley於1094年出生在英國英格蘭德比夏爾時，他的父親Nigel de Stafford26歲，母親Amicia de Ferrers24歲。他和Elena de Stafford至少有6個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1165年在英國斯塔福爾伯頓 武朋 特倫特享年71歲，埋葬在英國德比夏爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Stafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name principally from Stafford (Staffordshire), but occasionally from other places with similar names, such as Stafford House in Ifield (Sussex), possibly East and West Stowford in East Down (Devon), and three minor places in Devon called Stafford, in Dolton, Broadhembury, and Colyton parishes. The places are all named with Old English ford ‘ford’ as the final element, but have different initial elements. The Staffordshire placename has Old English stæth ‘river bank, shore’; Stafford in Colyton (Devon) has Old English stān ‘stone, rock’; the Sussex placename has Old English stēor ‘steer, bullock’; East and West Stowford (Devon) have Old English stæf ‘staff, stave, rod’; Stafford in Dolton and Stafford in Broadhembury (Devon) may have Old English stæth, stān, or stæf.

variants.name: Stocker
meanings: German and Swiss German (also Stöcker): topographic name for someone living by a tree stump (see Stock 3) or an occupational name for a tree cutter, from Middle High German stocken ‘to clear land’.
German and Swiss German (also Stöcker); Dutch: occupational name from Middle High German, Middle Dutch stocker ‘jailer’.

English: occupational name from Middle English stokker ‘one who sells stockfish’ (fish dried in the air without salt). This was the usual source of the name in medieval London, where a bylaw of 1419 stated that no stokker should board a ship to buy fish (presumably in order to forestall the market).

nameMeanings.name: Gresley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Greasley
meanings: 1  English: locative name, usually from Greasley (Notts) but possibly also from Gresley (Derbys). Some of the following early bearers may alternatively belong under (2), particularly the Lancs examples.

2 Norman, English: nickname, either from Old French greslet ‘marked as by hail’, i.e. pitted or pock-marked, or Old French greslet, gresli, grailet ‘thin, slim’. In early forms, the nickname has sometimes been confused with the place-name in (1). Compare Le Gresley in Jersey and Greslé, Grêlé in France.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When William FitzNigel de Stafford de Gresley was born in 1094, in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Nigel de Stafford, was 26 and his mother, Amicia de Ferrers, was 24. He had at least 6 sons and 3 daughters with Elena de Stafford. He died in 1165, in Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 71, and was buried in Derbyshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William FitzNigel de Stafford de Gresley est né en 1094, à Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Nigel de Stafford, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Amicia de Ferrers, avait 24 ans. Il a eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles avec Elena de Stafford. Il est décédé en 1165, à Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterré à Derbyshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William FitzNigel de Stafford de Gresley 1094 in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Nigel de Stafford 26 und seine Mutter Amicia de Ferrers 24. Er hatte mit Elena de Stafford mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1165 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, England und wurde in Derbyshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William FitzNigel de Stafford de Gresley è nato il nel 1094 nel Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Nigel de Stafford, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Amicia de Ferrers, aveva 24 anni. Lui avuto almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie con Elena de Stafford. Morì nel 1165, nel Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, England, all'età di 71 anni, e fu sepolto a Derbyshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William FitzNigel de Stafford de Gresleyが1094年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアで生まれた時，父Nigel de Staffordは26歳，母Amicia de Ferrersは24歳であった。彼はElena de Staffordとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1165年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアブルトン ウプオン トレントで71歳で死去し，イギリスデルブイシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William FitzNigel de Stafford de Gresley(이)가 1094년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Nigel de Stafford(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Amicia de Ferrers(은)는 24세였다. 그는 Elena de Stafford(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1165년 영국 스태퍼드실르 버턴 업 트렌트에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 더비실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William FitzNigel de Stafford de Gresley nasceu em 1094, em Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Nigel de Stafford, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Amicia de Ferrers, tinha 24 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas com Elena de Stafford. Ele faleceu em 1165, em Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, com 71 anos, e foi sepultado em Derbyshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William FitzNigel de Stafford de Gresley родился в 1094 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Nigel de Stafford было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Amicia de Ferrers, 24. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери от Elena de Stafford. Он умер в 1165 г. в местности буртон упон трент, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности дирбишир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William FitzNigel de Stafford de Gresley nació en 1094, en Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Nigel de Stafford, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Amicia de Ferrers, tenía 24 años. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas con Elena de Stafford. Murió en 1165, en Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultado en Derbyshire, Inglaterra.
